records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Ahmad al-Muqri from Yunis b. Dhabyan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Surah Buruj in his obligatory prayers, by virtue of the dignity of this Surah, he would be raised among the prophets because this Surah is related to them.”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ الْمُقْرِی عَنْ یُونُسَ بْنِ ظَبْیَانَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ وَ السَّماءِ ذاتِ الْبُرُوجِ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ فَإِنَّهَا سُورَهُ النَّبِیِّینَ کَانَ مَحْشَرُهُ وَ مَوْقِفُهُ مَعَ النَّبِیِّینَ وَ الْمُرْسَلِینَ وَ الصَّالِحِینَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Ahmad al-Muqri from Yunis b. Dhabyan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Buruj in his obligatory prayers, by virtue of the dignity of this Surah, he would be raised among the prophets because this Surah is related to them.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hussain from his father from al-Mu’ala b. Khunays from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Surah Tariq in his obligatory prayer would enjoy great prestige and position before Allah on the Day of Judgment and he would be among the friends of the prophets and their companions in Paradise.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ الْمُعَلَّی بْنِ خُنَیْسٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ کَانَتْ قِرَاءَتُهُ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ بِ السَّماءِ وَ الطَّارِقِ کَانَتْ لَهُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ جَاهٌ وَ مَنْزِلَهٌ وَ کَانَ مِنْ رُفَقَاءِ النَّبِیِّینَ وَ أَصْحَابِهِمْ فِی الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hussain from his father from al-Mu’ala b. Khunays from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Tariq in his obligatory prayer would enjoy great prestige and position before Allah on the Day of Judgment and he would be among the friends of the prophets and their companions in Paradise.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hussain from his father from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites it in his obligatory and recommended prayers, it would be said to him on the Day of Judgment: Enter Paradise from whichever door you like.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ سَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّکَ الْأَعْلَی فِی فَرِیضَهٍ أَوْ نَافِلَهٍ قِیلَ لَهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ ادْخُلِ الْجَنَّهَ مِنْ أَیِّ أَبْوَابِ الْجَنَّهِ شِئْتَ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hussain from his father from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites it in his obligatory and recommended prayers, it would be said to him on the Day of Judgment: Enter Paradise from whichever door you like.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Abi al-Mi’za from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: One who regularly recites: Surah Ghashiya in his obligatory and recommended prayers, Allah’s mercy would pervade him in this world as well as the next, and on the Day of Judgment, Allah would keep that man safe and protected from the chastisement of fire.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِی الْمِعْزَی عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَدْمَنَ قِرَاءَهَ هَلْ أَتاکَ حَدِیثُ الْغاشِیَهِ فِی فَرِیضَهٍ أَوْ نَافِلَهٍ غَشَّاهُ اللَّهُ بِرَحْمَتِهِ فِی الدُّنْیَا وَ الْآخِرَهِ وَ آتَاهُ اللَّهُ الْأَمْنَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ مِنْ عَذَابِ النَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Abi al-Mi’za from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: One who regularly recites: Surah Ghashiya in his obligatory and recommended prayers, Allah’s mercy would pervade him in this world as well as the next, and on the Day of Judgment, Allah would keep that man safe and protected from the chastisement of fire.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sandal from Dawud b. Farqad from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Recite Surah Fajr in your obligatory and recommended prayers. This Surah is related to Imam Husain bin Ali (a.s.). Hence the reciter of this Surah will get a position near Imam Husain (a.s.) in Paradise on the Day of Judgment. Surely Allah is Honored and Wise.”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ صَنْدَلٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ فَرْقَدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: اقْرَءُوا سُورَهَ الْفَجْرِ فِی فَرَائِضِکُمْ وَ نَوَافِلِکُمْ فَإِنَّهَا سُورَهٌ لِلْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ ع مَنْ قَرَأَهَا کَانَ مَعَ الْحُسَیْنِ ع یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ فِی دَرَجَتِهِ مِنَ الْجَنَّهِ- إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِیزٌ حَکِیمٌ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sandal from Dawud b. Farqad from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Recite Surah Fajr in your obligatory and recommended prayers. This Surah is related to Imam Husain bin Ali (a.s.). Hence the reciter of this Surah will get a position near Imam Husain (a.s.) in Paradise on the Day of Judgment. Surely Allah is Honored and Wise.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from his father and al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Surah Balad in his obligatory prayers will be a virtuous man in this world. He would be known on the Day of Judgment as a person of high rank near Allah. He would be a friend of prophets, martyrs and virtuous on the Day of Judgment.”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ وَ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ کَانَ قِرَاءَتُهُ فِی فَرِیضَهٍ لا أُقْسِمُ بِهذَا الْبَلَدِ کَانَ فِی الدُّنْیَا مَعْرُوفاً أَنَّهُ مِنَ الصَّالِحِینَ وَ کَانَ فِی الْآخِرَهِ مَعْرُوفاً أَنَّ لَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَکَاناً وَ کَانَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ مِنْ رُفَقَاءِ النَّبِیِّینَ وَ الشُّهَداءِ وَ الصَّالِحِینَ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from his father and al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Balad in his obligatory prayers will be a virtuous man in this world. He would be known on the Day of Judgment as a person of high rank near Allah. He would be a friend of prophets, martyrs and virtuous on the Day of Judgment.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Shams, Lail, Zuha and Alam Nashrah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Muawiyah b. Ammar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Surah Shams, Surah Lail, Surah Zuha and Surah Alam Nashrah in one day or night, nothing would remain that would not stand witness for his recitation on the Day of Judgment, so much so that the reciter’s hair, face, blood, muscles and even bones would stand witness for him, and everything that grows from the earth would stand witness along with them and Allah would say: I have accepted the evidence of all of you for My servant, now admit him into Paradise and give him all that he likes on My behalf. Along with this also give him more by virtue of My mercy and favor.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَکْثَرَ قِرَاءَهَ وَ الشَّمْسِ وَ وَ اللَّیْلِ إِذا یَغْشی وَ وَ الضُّحی وَ أَ لَمْ نَشْرَحْ فِی یَوْمٍ أَوْ لَیْلَهٍ لَمْ یَبْقَ شَیْ ءٌ بِحَضْرَتِهِ إِلَّا شَهِدَ لَهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ حَتَّی شَعْرُهُ وَ بَشَرُهُ وَ لَحْمُهُ وَ دَمُهُ وَ عُرُوقُهُ وَ عَصَبُهُ وَ عِظَامُهُ وَ جَمِیعُ مَا أَقَلَّتِ الْأَرْضُ مِنْهُ وَ یَقُولُ الرَّبُّ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی قَبِلْتُ شَهَادَتَکُمْ لِعَبْدِی وَ أَجَزْتُهَا لَهُ انْطَلِقُوا بِهِ إِلَی جِنَانِی حَتَّی یَتَخَیَّرَ مِنْهَا حَیْثُ مَا أَحَبَّ فَأَعْطُوهُ إِیَّاهَا مِنْ غَیْرِ مَنٍّ وَ لَکِنْ رَحْمَهً مِنِّی وَ فَضْلًا عَلَیْهِ وَ هَنِیئاً لِعَبْدِی.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Muawiyah b. Ammar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Shams, Surah Lail, Surah Zuha and Surah Alam Nashrah in one day or night, nothing would remain that would not stand witness for his recitation on the Day of Judgment, so much so that the reciter’s hair, face, blood, muscles and even bones would stand witness for him, and everything that grows from the earth would stand witness along with them and Allah would say: I have accepted the evidence of all of you for My servant, now admit him into Paradise and give him all that he likes on My behalf. Along with this also give him more by virtue of My mercy and favor.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Shu’aib al-’Aqraqufi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Surah Teen in his obligatory and recommended prayers would be given a place in Paradise of his liking, if Allah wills.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ شُعَیْبٍ الْعَقَرْقُوفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ وَ التِّینِ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ وَ نَوَافِلِهِ أُعْطِیَ مِنَ الْجَنَّهِ حَیْثُ یَرْضَی إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Shu’aib al-’Aqraqufi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Teen in his obligatory and recommended prayers would be given a place in Paradise of his liking, if Allah wills.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ali b. Muskan from Sulayman b. Khalid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: If someone recites Surah Alaq in the day or night and then he dies the same day or night it is as though he achieved the rank of martyrdom and he would remain alive like a martyr who might have used his sword along with the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.).
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مُسْکَانَ عَنْ سُلَیْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ فِی یَوْمِهِ أَوْ لَیْلَتِهِ اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّکَ ثُمَّ مَاتَ فِی یَوْمِهِ أَوْ لَیْلَتِهِ مَاتَ شَهِیداً وَ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ شَهِیداً وَ أَحْیَاهُ شَهِیداً وَ کَانَ کَمَنْ ضَرَبَ بِسَیْفِهِ فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ali b. Muskan from Sulayman b. Khalid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: If someone recites Surah Alaq in the day or night and then he dies the same day or night it is as though he achieved the rank of martyrdom and he would remain alive like a martyr who might have used his sword along with the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.).

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. Abi (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Sayf b. ’Umayra from a man from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Surah Qadr loudly is like a person who raises sword in the way of Allah and one who recites Surah Qadr in low voice is like a person who is smeared in blood fighting in the way of Allah. One who recites it ten times, Allah will forgive one thousand sins committed by him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِی لَیْلَهِ الْقَدْرِ مُجْهِراً بِهَا صَوْتَهُ کَانَ کَالشَّاهِرِ سَیْفَهُ فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ مَنْ قَرَأَهَا سِرّاً کَانَ کَمُتَشَحِّطٍ بِدَمِهِ فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ وَ مَنْ قَرَأَهَا عَشْرَ مَرَّاتٍ مَحَا اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَلْفَ ذَنْبٍ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. Abi (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Sayf b. ’Umayra from a man from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Qadr loudly is like a person who raises sword in the way of Allah and one who recites Surah Qadr in low voice is like a person who is smeared in blood fighting in the way of Allah. One who recites it ten times, Allah will forgive one thousand sins committed by him.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from his father al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Whoever recites it in his obligatory prayers Allah makes an announcer to announce: O servant of Allah all your past sins have been forgiven; now you pay attention to your deeds.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَاهُ فِی فَرِیضَهٍ مِنْ فَرَائِضِ اللَّهِ نَادَی مُنَادٍ یَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَکَ مَا مَضَی فَاسْتَأْنِفِ الْعَمَلَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from his father al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Whoever recites it in his obligatory prayers Allah makes an announcer to announce: O servant of Allah all your past sins have been forgiven; now you pay attention to your deeds.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) from Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Hassaan from Ismail b. Mihran from al-Hassan from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Abi Bakr al-Hadhrami from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: "Whoever recites Surah Bayyinah, is untouched by Polytheism and has entered the religion of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.), and on the Day of Judgment, Allah would raise him as a believer, and his accounting would be very easy."
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ أَبِی بَکْرٍ الْحَضْرَمِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ سُورَهَ لَمْ یَکُنْ کَانَ بَرِیئاً مِنَ الشِّرْکِ وَ أُدْخِلَ فِی دِینِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص وَ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مُؤْمِناً وَ حَاسَبَهُ حِساباً یَسِیراً
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) from Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Hassaan from Ismail b. Mihran from al-Hassan from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Abi Bakr al-Hadhrami from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever recites Surah Bayyinah, is untouched by Polytheism and has entered the religion of Muhammad (s.a.w.s.), and on the Day of Judgment, Allah would raise him as a believer, and his accounting would be very easy."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ali b. Ma’bad from his father from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Do not get tired of reciting Surah Iza Zulzilatul Ardh, because whoever recites it in his obligatory or recommended prayers would never face the calamity of an earthquake by Power of Allah and would not die because of earthquake or lightning, nor would his death occur through other calamities of the world. When he dies, it would be ordered that he be taken to Paradise, Allah, the Blessed, would say: My servant you are an ornament for My Paradise, so take your abode in it wherever you like, nothing of this place is inadmissible to you, nor would anything of this place be kept away from you.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: لَا تَمَلُّوا مِنْ قِرَاءَهِ إِذا زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ فَإِنَّ مَنْ کَانَتْ قِرَاءَتَهُ فِی نَوَافِلِهِ لَمْ یُصِبْهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِزَلْزَلَهٍ أَبَداً وَ لَمْ یَمُتْ بِهَا وَ لَا بِصَاعِقَهٍ وَ لَا بِآفَهٍ مِنْ آفَاتِ الدُّنْیَا فَإِذَا مَاتَ أُمِرَ بِهِ إِلَی الْجَنَّهِ فَیَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَبْدِی أَبَحْتُکَ جَنَّتِی فَاسْکُنْ مِنْهَا حَیْثُ شِئْتَ وَ هَوِیتَ لَا مَمْنُوعاً وَ لَا مَدْفُوعاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ali b. Ma’bad from his father from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Do not get tired of reciting Surah Iza Zulzilatul Ardh, because whoever recites it in his obligatory or recommended prayers would never face the calamity of an earthquake by Power of Allah and would not die because of earthquake or lightning, nor would his death occur through other calamities of the world. When he dies, it would be ordered that he be taken to Paradise, Allah, the Blessed, would say: My servant you are an ornament for My Paradise, so take your abode in it wherever you like, nothing of this place is inadmissible to you, nor would anything of this place be kept away from you.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from Abi Abdillah al-Muwafi [al-Mu’min] from Ibn Muskan from Sulayman b. Khalid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: One who recites it, would be with Ali (a.s.) on the Day of Judgment and would be among his special companions.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الموفی [الْمُؤْمِنِ] عَنِ ابْنِ مُسْکَانَ عَنْ سُلَیْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ سُورَهَ الْعَادِیَاتِ وَ أَدْمَنَ قِرَاءَتَهَا بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَعَ أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ خَاصَّهً وَ کَانَ فِی حَجْرِهِ وَ رُفَقَائِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from Abi Abdillah al-Muwafi [al-Mu’min] from Ibn Muskan from Sulayman b. Khalid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: One who recites it, would be with Ali (a.s.) on the Day of Judgment and would be among his special companions.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hussain from Ismail b. al-Zubayr from ’Amru b. Thabit from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Whoever recites this Surah would be kept safe by Allah from acting upon the mischief of Dajjal and he would remain safe from the chastisement of Hell.”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ الزُّبَیْرِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ ثَابِتٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ وَ أَکْثَرَ مِنْ قِرَاءَهِ الْقَارِعَهِ آمَنَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ فِتْنَهِ الدَّجَّالِ أَنْ یُؤْمِنَ بِهِ وَ مِنْ فَیْحِ جَهَنَّمَ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hussain from Ismail b. al-Zubayr from ’Amru b. Thabit from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Whoever recites this Surah would be kept safe by Allah from acting upon the mischief of Dajjal and he would remain safe from the chastisement of Hell.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sa’eed from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Al- Haakumut Takathur (Surah Takathur) in his obligatory Prayer, reward for a hundred martyrs would be written for him by command of Allah and whoever recites it in his recommended prayers, Allah would grant him reward for fifty martyrs. Further, if Allah wills, forty rows of angels would offer prayer with the reciter of Surah Takathur.
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ أَلْهاکُمُ التَّکاثُرُ فِی فَرِیضَهٍ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ثَوَابَ أَجْرِ مِائَهِ شَهِیدٍ وَ مَنْ قَرَأَهَا فِی نَافِلَهٍ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ثَوَابَ خَمْسِینَ شَهِیداً وَ صَلَّی مَعَهُ فِی فَرِیضَتِهِ أَرْبَعُونَ صَفّاً مِنَ الْمَلَائِکَهِ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sa’eed from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Al- Haakumut Takathur (Surah Takathur) in his obligatory Prayer, reward for a hundred martyrs would be written for him by command of Allah and whoever recites it in his recommended prayers, Allah would grant him reward for fifty martyrs. Further, if Allah wills, forty rows of angels would offer prayer with the reciter of Surah Takathur.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “One  who  recites  Surah Takathur before going to bed would remain safe from the chastisement of grave.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ بَشَّارٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الدِّهْقَانِ عَنْ دُرُسْتَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ قَرَأَ أَلْهاکُمُ التَّکاثُرُ عِنْدَ النَّوْمِ وُقِیَ مِنْ فِتْنَهِ الْقَبْرِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ismail b. Mihran from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Surah Asr in his optional prayers, Allah would raise him on the Day of Judgment with cheerful and smiling face and happy and cool eyes, and eventually he would be admitted into Paradise.
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ وَ الْعَصْرِ فِی نَوَافِلِهِ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ مُشْرِقاً وَجْهُهُ ضَاحِکاً سِنُّهُ قَرِیرَهً عَیْنُهُ حَتَّی یَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّهَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ismail b. Mihran from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Asr in his optional prayers, Allah would raise him on the Day of Judgment with cheerful and smiling face and happy and cool eyes, and eventually he would be admitted into Paradise.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites ‘Wailul Lekulle Humazah’ (Surah Humazah) in his obligatory prayers, Allah would remove poverty and destitution from him and make livelihood easy for him and also keep him safe from accidental or bad death.
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ وَیْلٌ لِکُلِّ هُمَزَهٍ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ أَبْعَدَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ الْفَقْرَ وَ جَلَبَ عَلَیْهِ الرِّزْقَ وَ یَدْفَعُ عَنْهُ مِیتَهَ السَّوْءِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites ‘Wailul Lekulle Humazah’ (Surah Humazah) in his obligatory prayers, Allah would remove poverty and destitution from him and make livelihood easy for him and also keep him safe from accidental or bad death.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Feel and Surah Quraish
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Surah Feel in his obligatory prayers all deserts, mountains and soil will bear witness that the person is regular in offering prayers. A call will be given out on the Day of Judgment, ‘You had said right about My servant and I have accepted your testimony. Make him enter Paradise on account of this testimony and do not account for his deeds because I love him and his deeds. “
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ أَ لَمْ تَرَ کَیْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّکَ شَهِدَ لَهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ کُلُّ سَهْلٍ وَ جَبَلٍ وَ مَدَرٍ بِأَنَّهُ کَانَ مِنَ الْمُصَلِّینَ وَ یُنَادِی لَهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ مُنَادٍ صَدَقْتُمْ عَلَی عَبْدِی قَبِلْتُ شَهَادَتَکُمْ لَهُ وَ عَلَیْهِ أَدْخِلُوهُ الْجَنَّهَ وَ لَا تُحَاسِبُوهُ فَإِنَّهُ مِمَّنْ أُحِبُّهُ وَ أُحِبُّ عَمَلَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Feel in his obligatory prayers all deserts, mountains and soil will bear witness that the person is regular in offering prayers. A call will be given out on the Day of Judgment, ‘You had said right about My servant and I have accepted your testimony. Make him enter Paradise on account of this testimony and do not account for his deeds because I love him and his deeds. “

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Feel and Surah Quraish
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Abi al-Mi’za from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Recite Surah Feel and Quraish together in a rakat of an obligatory prayer because they are considered a single Surah. Hence it is not permissible to recite only one of them in an obligatory prayer.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِی الْمِعْزَی عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَکْثَرَ قِرَاءَهَ لِإِیلافِ قُرَیْشٍ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ عَلَی مَرْکَبٍ مِنْ مَرَاکِبِ الْجَنَّهِ حَتَّی یَقْعُدَ عَلَی مَوَائِدِ النُّورِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ. قال مصنف هذا الکتاب ره من قرأ سوره الفیل فلیقرأ معها لإیلاف فی رکعه فریضه فإنهما جمیعا سوره واحده و لا یجوز التفرد بواحده منهما
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hassan from Abi al-Mi’za from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Recite Surah Feel and Quraish together in a rakat of an obligatory prayer because they are considered a single Surah. Hence it is not permissible to recite only one of them in an obligatory prayer.”

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ismail b. al-Zubayr from ’Amru b. Thabit from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Surah Maoon in his obligatory and optional prayers would be among those whose prayers and fasts are accepted by Allah, the Mighty and the Sublime, and that man would not be subjected to reckoning for whatever he has done in the world.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ الزُّبَیْرِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ ثَابِتٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ سُورَهَ أَ رَأَیْتَ الَّذِی یُکَذِّبُ بِالدِّینِ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ وَ نَوَافِلِهِ کَانَ فِیمَنْ قَبِلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ صَلَاتَهُ وَ صِیَامَهُ وَ لَمْ یُحَاسِبْهُ بِمَا کَانَ مِنْهُ فِی الْحَیَاهِ الدُّنْیَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Ismail b. al-Zubayr from ’Amru b. Thabit from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Maoon in his obligatory and optional prayers would be among those whose prayers and fasts are accepted by Allah, the Mighty and the Sublime, and that man would not be subjected to reckoning for whatever he has done in the world.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: Whoever recites Surah Kauthar in his obligatory and recommended prayers, Allah would give him to drink from Kauthar on the Day of Judgment and he would be of those who had been with the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) under the shade of the tree (of Paradise) Tooba.
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ کَانَ قِرَاءَتُهُ إِنَّا أَعْطَیْناکَ الْکَوْثَرَ فِی فَرَائِضِهِ وَ نَوَافِلِهِ سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْکَوْثَرِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ کَانَ مُحَدَّثُهُ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص فِی أَصْلِ طُوبَی.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from al-Hussain b. Abi al-’Ala from Abi Basir from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Kauthar in his obligatory and recommended prayers, Allah would give him to drink from Kauthar on the Day of Judgment and he would be of those who had been with the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) under the shade of the tree (of Paradise) Tooba.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Kafirun and Surah Tauheed
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam as-Sadiq (a.s.) said: Whoever recites Surah Kafiroon and Surah Tauheed in one of his obligatory prayers, Allah grants  forgiveness  for  him,  for  his  parents  and  for  his children,  and  if  his  name  is  written  in  the  register  of unfortunate and disobedient persons, then after erasing it He writes   in it the register of virtuous and happy people and Allah would grant him the death of a martyr and raise him on the Day of Judgment with the martyrs.
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ قَرَأَ قُلْ یا أَیُّهَا الْکافِرُونَ وَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ فِی فَرِیضَهٍ مِنَ الْفَرَائِضِ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَ لِوَالِدَیْهِ وَ مَا وَلَدَ وَ إِنْ کَانَ شَقِیّاً مُحِیَ مِنْ دِیوَانِ الْأَشْقِیَاءِ وَ أُثْبِتَ فِی دِیوَانِ السُّعَ��َاءِ وَ أَحْیَاهُ اللَّهُ سَعِیداً وَ أَمَاتَهُ شَهِیداً وَ بَعَثَهُ شَهِیداً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam as-Sadiq (a.s.) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever recites Surah Kafiroon and Surah Tauheed in one of his obligatory prayers, Allah grants  forgiveness  for  him,  for  his  parents  and  for  his children,  and  if  his  name  is  written  in  the  register  of unfortunate and disobedient persons, then after erasing it He writes   in it the register of virtuous and happy people and Allah would grant him the death of a martyr and raise him on the Day of Judgment with the martyrs.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Aban b. Abd al-Malik b. Karram al-Khath’ami from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Surah Nasr in his obligatory or recommended prayers Allah will give him conquest over all enemies and he will have a talking book with him on the Day of Judgment. He will have protection against the bridge, fire and fiery voices of Hell when Allah resurrects him from grave. Every person meeting him will congratulate and give him glad tidings till he enters Paradise. A number of doors of goodness will be opened for him in this world so much that he would never have imagined or desired.”
records.arabicText: بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِکِ بْنِ کَرَّامٍ الْخَثْعَمِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ إِذا جاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَ الْفَتْحُ فِی نَافِلَهٍ أَوْ فَرِیضَهٍ نَصَرَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَی جَمِیعِ أَعْدَائِهِ وَ جَاءَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ مَعَهُ کِتَابٌ یَنْطِقُ قَدْ أَخْرَجَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ جَوْفِ قَبْرِهِ فِیهِ أَمَانٌ مِنْ جِسْرِ جَهَنَّمَ وَ مِنَ النَّارِ وَ مِنْ زَفِیرِ جَهَنَّمَ فَلَا یَمُرُّ عَلَی شَیْ ءٍ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ إِلَّا بَشَّرَهُ وَ أَخْبَرَهُ بِکُلِّ خَیْرٍ حَتَّی یَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّهَ وَ یُفْتَحُ لَهُ فِی الدُّنْیَا مِنْ أَسْبَابِ الْخَیْرِ مَا لَمْ یَتَمَنَّ وَ لَمْ یَخْطُرْ عَلَی قَلْبِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Aban b. Abd al-Malik b. Karram al-Khath’ami from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Surah Nasr in his obligatory or recommended prayers Allah will give him conquest over all enemies and he will have a talking book with him on the Day of Judgment. He will have protection against the bridge, fire and fiery voices of Hell when Allah resurrects him from grave. Every person meeting him will congratulate and give him glad tidings till he enters Paradise. A number of doors of goodness will be opened for him in this world so much that he would never have imagined or desired.”
